channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australians Consider Spending Cuts Amid Potential Interest Rate Hike
item.description: As the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) mulls over another potential interest rate increase, many Australians are already considering where they might reduce their spending. In a recent street survey c...

item.title: SAPS involvement in arrest of social media blogger Musa Khawula discussed at Madlanga commission
item.description: During proceedings at the Madlanga Commission of Inquiry into Criminality, Political Interference and Corruption in the Criminal Justice System, police officer Fannie Nkosi, also known as Witness F, r...

item.title: US Opens Trade Probe into South Africa and Other Nations Over Labour Concerns
item.description: The United States has launched a formal trade investigation into South Africa and 59 other countries over allegations of unfair trade practices, particularly the use of forced labour in the production...

item.title: Government allocates funds to complete stalled Muhimbili National Hospital private ward
item.description: The Tanzanian government has set aside 4 billion shillings to continue construction of the four-floor private ward at Muhimbili National Hospital, a project worth 21 billion shillings. Deputy Minister...

item.title: Pakistan Confirms Cooperation with China on Afghan Taliban Dialogue Amid Border Tensions
item.description: The Foreign Office (FO) of Pakistan on Monday categorically rejected media reports suggesting that Pakistan had refused China’s offer to mediate dialogue with the Afghan Taliban. FO spokesperson Tahir...

item.title: Key Maritime Chokepoints That Shape Global Trade
item.description: Global trade is highly reliant on a small number of narrow maritime passages known as chokepoints, whose disruption can have wide-reaching economic consequences. The current crisis in the Strait of Ho...

item.title: Court Hears Emefiele Not Directly Involved in Accounts of Companies Linked to Alleged CBN Contract Misconduct
item.description: During the FCT High Court session in Abuja on Monday, EFCC witness David Jaiyeoba clarified say former Central Bank governor, Godwin Emefiele, no be signatory, director, nor shareholder for the accoun...

item.title: Global Nations Cautious After Trump Urges Naval Escorts Through Strait of Hormuz Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: Countries around the world have reacted cautiously to former US President Donald Trump's call for naval escorts through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global oil exports. Iran has str...

item.title: TTCL introduces high-speed Faiba internet to improve connectivity across Tanzania
item.description: TTCL, the national telecommunications company, imeanza rasmi huduma yake mpya ya intaneti ya kasi sana inayoitwa 'Faiba Supersonic Experience; Kasi Balaa'. Lengo la huduma hii ni kuongezea upatikanaji...

item.title: Ontario to Remove Provincial HST on Newly Built Homes to Stimulate Housing Market
item.description: The Ontario government, led by Premier Doug Ford, is preparing to expand a tax relief program for homebuyers, removing the provincial portion of the Harmonized Sales Tax (HST) on all newly constructed...

item.title: Accountability Court Approves NAB's Request to Close Chaudhry Sugar Mills Investigation Against Nawaz Sharif and Maryam Nawaz
item.description: In a significant development, an accountability court in Lahore has granted approval to the National Accountability Bureau (NAB) to discontinue its probe in the Chaudhry Sugar Mills case involving for...

item.title: White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer, to continue in role during treatment
item.description: President Donald Trump has publicly announced that his White House Chief of Staff, Susie Wiles, has been diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer. In a post on Truth Social, Trump shared the news, des...

item.title: Young Models Recruited in Southeast Asia for AI-Assisted Scam Operations
item.description: Recent investigations reveal that organized scam operations in Southeast Asia are recruiting young people, primarily women, as 'AI face models' to assist in online fraud schemes. Applicants submit vid...

item.title: CBSE Class 12 Economics 2026 Exam: Key Topics and Revision Tips
item.description: The CBSE Class 12 Economics Board Exam 2026 is approaching, and students are advised to focus on crucial topics and concepts for effective preparation. Although the full article could not be accessed ...

item.title: Nairobi County gears up for emergency flood response
item.description: Nairobi Governor Johnson Sakaja has directed county officials to prepare a 48-hour action plan to tackle flooding, broken drainage systems, and damaged infrastructure across Nairobi. The directive fol...

item.title: Pakistan Confirms Adequate Fuel Stocks for March and Early April Amid Global Supply Challenges
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The government has assured that Pakistan’s fuel stocks are sufficient to meet the requirements for March and are expected to last up to mid-April. This was conveyed in a meeting of the comm...

item.title: How Social Work Supports the Development of Future Women Leaders
item.description: Every year on 17 March, World Social Work Day offers a moment to reflect on a profession that profoundly shapes individuals, families, and communities, often quietly. The 2026 theme, 'Co-Building Hope...

item.title: Autumn Durald Arkapaw Makes History with Best Cinematography Oscar Win
item.description: Autumn Durald Arkapaw has made history at the 98th Academy Awards by winning the Oscar for Best Cinematography, an achievement that highlights both her exceptional skill and the increasing recognition...

item.title: Daily Superquiz and Mini Crossword for 17 March 2026
item.description: The Age offers readers a daily Superquiz and Mini Crossword for Tuesday, 17 March 2026. The article explains that users can engage with a bite-sized puzzle featuring ten clues across twenty-five squar...

item.title: Nigerian Army raids Abia drug den, arrests three suspected dealers
item.description: On Monday, March 16, 2026, Nigerian Army personnel carried out a targeted raid on a notorious drug den in Umuahia, Abia State. The operation was led by troops from the 145 Battalion (Rear) along New R...
